intro_text: Here on the plains, the reaches of c_n's territory are vast, particularly in the direction p_l and {PRONOUN/p_l/poss} patrol are heading, where there is endless grass (under the blanket of snow) but no permanent water sources.
decline_text: The patrol is interrupted by a hunting patrol that needs help carrying prey back to camp — border marking will wait til later.

success_outcomes.text: The snow is disturbed where great towering herbivores have pulled it off to get at the grass underneath. p_l makes note of it. The smaller plant eaters will feast on the leftovers of the big ones. This might make a useful hunting ground, if c_n can get to it before the next snowfall.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to scouted good hunting grounds on patrol together.

success_outcomes.text: s_c isn't just talented at running fast — {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/have/has} some very good advice about moving in snow too and {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/share/shares} {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} tips for endurance. The other cats purr gratitude at the end of the day. Everyone's exhausted, but they made it the entire length of that far off border thanks to s_c.

log.cats_from: cat_from learned about running endurance from cat_to.
cat_from and cat_to made the long trek to the outer plains while on patrol together.

success_outcomes.text: s_c notices r_c beginning to shiver as {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} {VERB/r_c/trudge/trudges} with puffed out fur. {PRONOUN/s_c/subject/CAP} take the next opportunity to herd the patrol under the cover of a rock with a scrappy bush clinging to it. There's no reason to risk frostbite here, time to warm up.

log.cats_from: cat_from was grateful that cat_to noticed {PRONOUN/cat_from/object} struggling in the cold and found some shelter.
cat_from and cat_to made the long trek to the outer plains while on patrol together.

fail_outcomes.text: They trek, and they wander, and they amble, and they pad. But as the sun creeps over the sky, before they reach the far border itself, p_l spots r_c limping. It's nothing, {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} {VERB/r_c/say/says}, just slept on the leg wrong. But far from camp isn't the right place to be limping, and p_l calls the patrol off.

log.cats_from: cat_to noticed that cat_from was struggling on patrol and decided to take the patrol back to camp early.

fail_outcomes.text: Before they reach the far border itself, p_l spots s_c limping. It's nothing, {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/say/says}, and refuses to stop. But in these far reaches, in territory not commonly checked, other predators intrude. When a young, inexperienced cougar charges the patrol, s_c should have been able to run fast enough to escape. Should.

history_text.death: This cat was killed after continuing to patrol the far border while injured by a young, inexperienced cougar.

fail_outcomes.text: p_l really pushes them to be able to patrol all the far border in a single patrol. They make it, and are congratulated on the achievement when they come back into camp after dark, panting, but everyone who went out in the snow is more tired than they should be, and some have clearly gotten frostbite.

history_text.scar: m_c carried the marks of frostbite on {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} pelt from a freezing border patrol in leaf-bare.
history_text.death: m_c died from frostbite after a freezing border patrol in leaf-bare.

log.cats_from: cat_from approved of how cat_to pushed {PRONOUN/cat_to/poss} patrol to reach the far border.
cat_from disapproved of how cat_to pushed {PRONOUN/cat_to/poss} patrol to reach the far border.
cat_to pushed cat_from to finish a long patrol in the extreme cold, and the patrol suffered for it.

fail_outcomes.text: p_l really pushes them to be able to patrol all the far border in a single patrol. But s_c brings everything to a halt, spotting that some of the patrol are beginning to feel the effects of the cold, and insisting they return home. It saves anyone else from being harmed by the cold.

history_text.scar: m_c was scarred after a chill caught on patrol progressed to frostbite.
history_text.death: m_c caught a chill on patrol and never recovered.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to advocated for {PRONOUN/cat_to/poss} Clanmates and urged p_l to end a patrol early when the cold took its toll on the cats.
cat_from disapproved of cat_to ending a patrol early just because it was cold that day.
cat_from doubted cat_to's leadership when {PRONOUN/cat_to/subject} tried to force a patrol to persevere in the extreme cold.
cat_from was very grateful that cat_to spoke up on behalf of the patrol when the cold started to take its toll on them.

intro_text: Padding along the border, the cats find fresh coyote scat, steaming in the snow. There's a yip in the distance.
decline_text: The patrol decides not to pursue such a dangerous creature, especially seeing as it hasn't crossed the border.

success_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. None notice the cats, but the patrol looks at each other with dismay — c_n really doesn't need a breeding pair setting up near them, they need to report this back straight away.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol reported courting coyotes on their territory.

success_outcomes.text: The patrol shadows the coyote, sending a message back to warn camp. It's an exhausting and stressful day, but by the time night falls they've confirmed that the coyote is leaving the local area, and the patrol has assured c_n's safety.

log.cats_from: cat_from appreciated how cat_to's patrol shadowed a coyote to track its movements and keep c_n safe.

success_outcomes.text: There's a lone oak near here. p_l darts towards it with the patrol, scrambling up. Not a moment too soon — from their perch in the oak, the patrol hears the scrabbling of paws and a yip of frustration. That confirms the coyote exists and is interested in cat meat. They wait for the predator to give up, so they can go warn c_n.

log.cats_from: cat_from appreciated cat_to warning the Clan about a coyote.
cat_from gained respect for cat_to when {PRONOUN/cat_to/subject} thought fast and led the patrol up a tree to escape a coyote.

success_outcomes.text: The patrol gathers their courage and valiantly tries to lead away the coyote. With such a small group of cats, they can only lead it a short distance, but it's better than nothing.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard about how cat_to's small patrol led a coyote away from the camp.

success_outcomes.text: s_c sets up a careful ambush ahead of the coyote, and the surprising, darting attack bloodies the coyote's flank, dissuading it from heading further into c_n territory.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard about how cat_to's patrol attacked a coyote to drive it out of the territory.
cat_to ambushed a coyote on patrol and led cat_from in an effort to drive it off the territory.

success_outcomes.text: With the border here so flat, there's no trees to climb to escape. But s_c thinks of everything; they go to a well-known burrow that the apprentices play in all the time. It's not a moment too soon, the coyote wanders straight past the entrance as it hunts for field mice, the cats within crouched and silent.

log.cats_from: cat_to saved cat_from from a coyote by finding a burrow for the patrol to hide in.

success_outcomes.text: That isn't just the sound of a coyote, that's the sound of a coyote calling for a mate, s_c hisses. It's not good, it means there'll be males attracted in by that female, and the cats return home quickly and silently. c_n needs to know about the danger in the borderlands.

log.cats_from: cat_from appreciated cat_to warning the Clan of courting coyotes.
cat_to identified the sound of courting coyotes and warned the patrol before they encountered danger.

success_outcomes.text: s_c selflessly takes the lion's share of the run as the patrol leads the coyote on, exhausting {PRONOUN/s_c/self} almost to the point of danger, but leading the coyote far away from c_n borders. {PRONOUN/s_c/poss/CAP} Clanmates support {PRONOUN/s_c/object} on the long walk home once it's all over, their purrs mixing with s_c's exhausted pants.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to led a coyote away from c_n territory on a patrol.
cat_from was grateful that cat_to took on the brunt of the danger in leading a coyote away from c_n territory.

fail_outcomes.text: Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer and fans out, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. One offers a gift to the female, and p_l's stomach drops. {PRONOUN/r_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_c/were/was} only out of sight for seconds — but r_c won't be coming home.

history_text.death: m_c was killed by a coyote male trying to offer a courting gift to a female.

log.cats_from: cat_to laid down {PRONOUN/cat_to/poss} life to protect c_n from coyotes.

fail_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. One discovers r_c and decides that cat would make a nice courting gift. The patrol runs in to save {PRONOUN/r_c/object}, finding refuge crammed in a rabbit burrow as the coyotes howl outside.

history_text.scar: Courting coyotes scarred m_c's pelt.
history_text.death: m_c died of {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} injuries after a coyote male tried to offer {PRONOUN/m_c/object} as a courting gift to a female.

fail_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. One discovers r_c, and decides that cat would make a nice courting gift. s_c runs in to save {PRONOUN/r_c/object}, fighting like an entire patrol just by {PRONOUN/s_c/self}.

history_text.scar: Driving off coyotes left its scars on r_c.
history_text.death: r_c died from injuries sustained in keeping coyotes away from c_n's borders.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to held off a coyote long enough to save the lives of the rest of the patrol.
cat_to held off a coyote long enough to save the lives of cat_from and the rest of the patrol.

fail_outcomes.text: The patrol tries to keep watch on the coyote from a distance, but it spots the cats and they have to scatter. Everyone escapes just fine, but they lose track of the coyote. It could be anywhere still out there, lurking, waiting to attack.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to failed to track a coyote on patrol, leaving c_n in danger.

fail_outcomes.text: s_c tries to take the lion's share of the effort to lead the coyote away, but {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} strength fails {PRONOUN/s_c/object} and the rest of the patrol needs to rescue {PRONOUN/s_c/object}, cutting short the results of the patrol. Instead, everyone knows the coyote could still be lurking on their borders.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to failed to track a coyote on patrol, leaving c_n in danger.

fail_outcomes.text: The patrol tries to lead the coyote away from the Clan's borders, but with so few cats it's impossible to cover each other's backs. r_c falls to the coyote as the rest of the patrol runs for their lives back to camp, terrified out of their pelts and mourning their Clanmate.

history_text.death: m_c died to keep the Clan safe from a large coyote.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol wasn't able to lead a coyote away and r_c was killed as a result.

fail_outcomes.text: The patrol tries bravely to lead the coyote away from c_n borders, but with so few cats it's impossible to cover each other's backs. r_c gets caught while running, and it's all the rest of the patrol can do to distract the coyote long enough to get {PRONOUN/r_c/object} to safety.

history_text.scar: m_c was scarred defending the Clan against a coyote.
history_text.death: m_c died of injuries inflicted by a coyote.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol wasn't able to lead a coyote away and r_c was injured as a result.

intro_text: Approaching the border, the patrol stops in their tracks. A coyote has marked over c_n's border markings — so recently that it stinks from several fox-lengths away, sharply disgusting in the muffled frosty world, so recently that the canine might be still here.
decline_text: The patrol decides not to pursue such a dangerous creature. c_n will just have to hope it doesn't come any nearer to camp.

success_outcomes.text: The warriors share a glance and a nod. They have enough cats to try and tackle this problem. Spreading out, they use a search pattern they've all been trained in to isolate the adult coyote's location, and the cover of the snow drifts to approach stealthily. r_c starts the battle strong, sinking {PRONOUN/r_c/poss} teeth into the coyote's hamstring, and the rest of the patrol break cover with screeches, driving the coyote away from c_n.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol drove off a coyote.
cat_to's daring move helped cat_from's patrol drive off a coyote.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to drive off a coyote.

success_outcomes.text: s_c uses {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} tracking talents to lead the patrol to their enemy. They find the coyote huddled up, tail tucked over its running nose, with prey stolen from c_n lying at its feet while it warms up from the frost. Not only does the patrol manage to maul the canine, they also recapture the food.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol drove off a coyote.
cat_to led cat_from and the rest of the patrol in tracking down a coyote.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to drive off a coyote.

success_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. None notice the cats, but the patrol looks at each other with dismay. It's extremely dangerous to attack so many coyotes at once, even with a large border patrol. They report back to camp instead.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol reported courting coyotes on their territory.

success_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. None notice the cats, but the patrol looks at each other with dismay. It's extremely dangerous to attack so many coyotes at once, even with a large border patrol, but s_c leads them to attack anyway, and miraculously, the cats drive the pack away from c_n.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol, unbelievably, drove off an entire pack of coyotes.
cat_to led cat_from and the rest of the patrol in driving off an entire pack of coyotes.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to drive off an entire pack of coyotes.

success_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. None notice the cats, but the patrol looks at each other with dismay. Because s_c is with them, sprinting as fast as the wind in the worst of this life-threatening relay race, the patrol is able to lead the stinking pack away.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol led a pack of coyotes off the territory.
cat_to's speed allowed cat_from's patrol to lead a pack of coyotes away from c_n's territory.
cat_from and cat_to lead a pack of coyotes away from c_n's territory.

success_outcomes.text: They decide that no cat is going to be surprised by this predator again, and the patrol shadows the coyote, sending a message to warn camp. With enough cats to keep watch, rest, track, and send messages forward and back from camp, c_n is kept safe from the wandering coyote.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol tracked a coyote.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to track a coyote.

success_outcomes.text: The patrol gathers their courage and valiantly drives away the coyote. Together, working as one, and with all the strength of StarClan behind them, the patrol presses the coyote. By keeping up a series of small skirmishes through the entire day, they drive it far off c_n territory.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol drove off a coyote.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to drive off a coyote.

success_outcomes.text: A yip sounds from close by. s_c has just enough time to yell out a plan, leading the whole large patrol in a skillful battle plan against the coyote that protects the cats from the chance of injury. The coyote tucks tail, and runs for more friendly lands.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol drove off a coyote.
cat_from's patrol was able to drive off a coyote thanks to cat_to's brilliant strategy.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to drive off a coyote.

success_outcomes.text: s_c selflessly throws {PRONOUN/s_c/self} into the fight, and every time the coyote tries to grab at {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} Clanmates, {PRONOUN/s_c/subject}{VERB/s_c/'re/'s} there with claws, fur puffed out to twice {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} normal size. With s_c defending them, the patrol drives the coyote off, yowling triumphantly.

log.cats_from: cat_from heard that cat_to's patrol drove off a coyote.
cat_to led cat_from's patrol in a fight against a coyote.
cat_from and cat_to worked together to drive off a coyote.

fail_outcomes.text: The warriors share a glance and a nod. They have enough cats to try and tackle this problem. Spreading out, they use a search pattern they've all been trained in to isolate the adult coyote's location, and the cover of the snow to approach stealthily. But r_c misjudges the first attack, and instead the patrol has to focus on defence and getting out of there mostly unscathed.

cat_from heard that cat_to's foolhardy attack on a pack of coyotes caused the deaths and injuries of multiple Clanmates.

fail_outcomes.text: That yip seemed a little weird. Ears pricked, the patrol stalks closer, crossing the border. On the other side, a female assesses the males trying to court her. None notice the cats, but the patrol looks at each other with dismay. The patrol tries to lead the pack away with a relay of cats distracting them, but r_c gets caught by a male's jaws, and it's only s_c's first aid that saves {PRONOUN/r_c/object} from dying in the snow.

history_text.death: r_c died from injuries sustained keeping coyotes away from c_n's borders.

log.cats_from: cat_to saved cat_from's life on patrol by staunching the blood-flow after a coyote took a bite out of {PRONOUN/cat_from/object}.

fail_outcomes.text: The patrol tries to keep watch on the coyote from a distance, but it spots the cats and they have to scatter. It's not as bad as it could've been though; r_c manages to keep track of the coyote's position, and it doesn't come any further into c_n territory.

s_c tries to show off {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} strength as the patrol bounds toward battle, but it gets in the way of the attack. Without everyone committed to driving the coyote off, the patrol has to fall back, but they do so in good order.

log.cats_from: cat_from blamed cat_to for the patrol's failure to drive off a coyote.

fail_outcomes.text: With so many cats all working as one, the coyote started to get beaten back. But just as the tide of the fight seems about to turn, one lucky strike hits r_c, and those vicious jaws take {PRONOUN/r_c/object} to StarClan.

history_text.death: m_c died to keep the Clan safe from a large coyote.

log.cats_from: cat_from honored cat_to's noble death to keep the Clan safe from coyotes.

fail_outcomes.text: The patrol fights bravely, but you only need to be unlucky once. r_c gets taken out of the fight with one snap of the beast's huge jaws. The rest of the patrol rallies to defend {PRONOUN/r_c/object}, and r_c is safely helped back to camp, where the medicine den awaits.

history_text.scar: m_c was scarred defending the Clan against a coyote.
history_text.death: m_c died of injuries inflicted by a coyote.

log.cats_from: cat_from respected that cat_to put {PRONOUN/cat_to/self} in danger in a fight against a coyote.

intro_text: Patrolling the border and updating the markings, p_l feels the temperature drop. Rearing up, {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} can just make out the distant sky above the valley of trampled snow the patrol pads in. A wall of gray approaches. They need to take cover!
decline_text: Fortunately they haven't padded far from camp yet, and the patrol makes it back before the storm hits.

success_outcomes.text: p_l knows a set of tunnels near here and charges through the snowbanks, leading the patrol to the entrance and digging frantically as the wind starts to pick up. They make it inside by the skin of their teeth, but the important thing is that they make it.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a tunnel together.
cat_to's quick thinking allowed cat_from and the patrol to find shelter before a snowstorm hit.

success_outcomes.text: Fox-dung. Controlling the urge to panic, s_c takes off, running to the shelter {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/were/was} keeping in the back of {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} mind when the patrol set out this morning and leading the way for the rest of the cats. It's not paranoia when the weather really is out to get you.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a tunnel together.
cat_to's cautious nature allowed cat_from and the patrol to find shelter before a snowstorm hit.

success_outcomes.text: It's important not to panic. s_c takes one deep breath. Two. By {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} third, {PRONOUN/s_c/subject}{VERB/s_c/'ve/'s} remembered the route to the nearest set of known, safe tunnels. The rest of the patrol is impatient, but they all apologize later, packed snugly and safely into an old badger sett as the storm rages outside.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a tunnel together.
cat_to remembered the route to a tunnel where cat_from and the patrol could shelter before a snowstorm hit.

success_outcomes.text: s_c is an expert on the network of tunnels and burrows that dot the plains, and the entire patrol immediately looks to {PRONOUN/s_c/object}. {PRONOUN/s_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/s_c/nod/nods} — {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/know/knows} just the place, an old sett. By the smell of the wind, the storm blowing down on them can wick away all warmth from a pelt. The patrol needs to shelter and wait it out.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a tunnel together.
cat_to's knowledge of the burrows and tunnels on the territory allowed cat_from and the patrol to find shelter before a snowstorm hit.

success_outcomes.text: The storm hits minutes before the patrol finds the entrance to some tunnels to shelter in. Once inside, s_c instructs everyone to start sharing tongues. They all need to get their pelts dry and warm again, as soon as possible.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a tunnel together.
cat_to encouraged cat_from and the patrol to share tongues to stay warm during a snowstorm.

success_outcomes.text: s_c spots a rabbit bolting for home. That'll do. Calling the patrol in, s_c tracks it to its burrow, and the cats cram themselves in, one at a time and completely squished, to wait out the storm. It's not comfortable, but it works. At least they don't go hungry.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a warren together.
cat_to's quick thinking allowed cat_from and the patrol to find shelter in a warren before a snowstorm hit.

success_outcomes.text: There's absolutely no shelter here, no tunnels, no burrow, no bushes or valleys, nothing. The patrol begins to panic, but s_c frowns, concentrating, remembering the histories of cats who live on hills of ice. The snow itself can be their den: a snow-den. Cold, but better than nothing.

history_text.scar: m_c was caught out in the cold on a border patrol, and the cold burned {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.
history_text.death: Though m_c's patrol found shelter during a snowstorm, m_c still caught a chill that eventually killed {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to hid from a snowstorm in a tunnel together.
cat_to's knowledge of cats who live in hills of ice gave {PRONOUN/cat_to/object} the idea to shelter in a snow-den with cat_from and the rest of the patrol.

fail_outcomes.text: No one can remember any tunnels near this stretch of border, and the patrol huddles under a bush instead. The storm hits, not with snow or hail, but with dreadful, bone-chilling sleet that sucks the heat from the cats' pelts and slicks their fur against the skin.

history_text.scar: m_c was caught out in the cold on a border patrol, and the cold burned {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.
history_text.death: m_c died of {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} injuries when {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} border patrol failed to find shelter in a sleet storm.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to were unable to find shelter in a snowstorm and lost trust in each other's skills.

fail_outcomes.text: No one can remember any tunnels near this stretch of border, and the patrol huddles under a bush instead. The storm hits, not with snow or hail, but with dreadful, bone-chilling sleet that sucks the heat from the cats' pelts and slicks their fur against the skin. r_c becomes unresponsive.

history_text.scar: m_c was caught out in the cold on a border patrol, and the cold burned {PRONOUN/m_c/object}.
history_text.death: m_c died of {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} injuries when {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} border patrol failed to find shelter in a sleet storm.

intro_text: While on a dawn border patrol, with the sun still hugging the distant horizon, p_l finds an opening to what seems to be a new set of tunnels.
decline_text: The patrol decides not to check the tunnels. After all, who knows what might be hiding inside?

success_outcomes.text: This could be a burrow, r_c agrees. But digging it out to check will chill their paws, and possibly disturb a hibernating occupant, if they're unlucky. Reporting it back at camp is worth doing, whereas poking at StarClan-knows-what under the snow might be dangerous.

r_c congratulates p_l on the discovery — this'll be welcome news back at camp. But once p_l turns away, {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} can't help the small twitches at the end of {PRONOUN/r_c/poss} tail. {PRONOUN/r_c/subject/CAP} {VERB/r_c/were/was} so close to finding those tunnels {PRONOUN/r_c/self}! If only r_c had spotted hole, half buried and disguised in the snow.

cat_from heard that cat_to discovered a new set of tunnels on the territory.

success_outcomes.text: These aren't new, but the smell of badger on them is. Something's very odd here. No badger, no matter the species, should be moving into new tunnels during leaf-bare. Maybe it's injured, or sick, maybe it was driven out of its cete, but they should report this to the rest of c_n.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to found a badger's scent around a tunnel on patrol and went back to camp to report it.

success_outcomes.text: These aren't new, but the smell of them is. Something's weird here, possibly a badger? Not a big one, both cats agree, but one of the smaller variety, though what it's doing so far from its cete is a mystery. That kind typically moves in packs. s_c devises a plan to block the entrance with snow, to mark the place for another, bigger border patrol to deal with.

log.cats_from: cat_from approved of cat_to's plan to block a badger sett's entrance with snow.

A rainstorm rolls in, too warm for snow or sleet but too cold to be out walking in. p_l and app1 shelter in their newly found refuge.

Perhaps because this is too shallow to be a refuge from predators?

This burrow is weird. app1's never seen a tunnel with straight walls, or metal ones, for that matter. Pelt standing up in a little mohawk along {PRONOUN/app1/poss} spine, {PRONOUN/app1/subject} {VERB/app1/back/backs} away.

history_text.scar: m_c was injured after disturbing a wrathful prairie dog.
history_text.death: m_c died of injuries inflicted by a prairie dog.

fail_outcomes.text: This burrow is weird. app1's never seen a tunnel with straight walls, or metal ones, for that matter. But c_n can always benefit from knowing about new tunnels, so {PRONOUN/app1/subject} {VERB/app1/creep/creeps} in. The trap door slams shut behind {PRONOUN/app1/object}.

This is going to be such a cool story to tell back at camp, app1 tells {PRONOUN/app1/self}. Checking the tunnel out, it looks like a collapsed roof into a wider tunnel system, smelling faintly of something app1 can't identify. Huh, app1 wonders what-

history_text.scar: m_c was injured after disturbing a wrathful prairie dog.
history_text.death: This cat was killed as an apprentice, patrolling the border alone and investigating things {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} shouldn't have.

fail_outcomes.text: Okay, so that might be a tunnel. app1 should go check it out, right? c_n cares about tunnels and stuff. {PRONOUN/app1/subject/CAP} {VERB/app1/clamber/clambers} in, and then there's a sharp pain.

history_text.death: This cat was killed as an apprentice, patrolling the border alone and investigating things {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} shouldn't have.

fail_outcomes.text: The shallow cave is home to a young badger, inexperienced, far from its natal family, and dangerous. p_l escapes, battered, bruised, and bleeding, but the subadult predator won't follow p_l back to camp, not in territory that's unfamiliar and new to it.

history_text.scar: m_c was injured after disturbing a badger in its sett.
history_text.death: m_c died of injuries inflicted by a badger.

fail_outcomes.text: s_c rushes into the tunnels with a gleeful meow! A new place to explore and discover! The wolverine inside is less pleased to have company.

history_text.scar: m_c was injured after disturbing a wolverine in its sett.
history_text.death: This cat was killed after an unfortunate meeting with a wolverine.

intro_text: The apprentices are at the border — not exactly a patrol they were sent on. Not exactly a patrol they're supposed to do without a warrior, actually. Not a patrol anyone is supposed to do with a storm rolling in. Anyway, is that a tunnel entrance over there?
decline_text: Eventually the apprentices get tired. Whatever, they're not supposed to be out here without a warrior anyway.

success_outcomes.text: They don't know this tunnel — but it knows them, marked by scent and claw as one of the safe refuges in the network c_n constantly maintains and expands in their territory. It knows them, and it saves them, because while they explore it the storm breaks outside, and what was a fun adventure becomes a little more scary as they huddle underground to escape the cold.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to had fun exploring a tunnel together, then ended up sheltering in it when a snowstorm descended.

success_outcomes.text: app1 claims the discovery for {PRONOUN/app1/self}, shoving app2 aside with {PRONOUN/app1/poss} shoulder when app2 comes to take a look. What a furball, app2 isn't staying here to be treated like this, {PRONOUN/app2/subject}{VERB/app2/'re/'s} going home!

log.cats_from: cat_from was upset when cat_to shoved {PRONOUN/cat_from/object} aside to claim the discovery of a tunnel for {PRONOUN/cat_to/self}.

success_outcomes.text: app1 has found a wonderful set of tunnels, and just in time. Everyone cowers low to the ground as thunder sweeps the plains, sending the apprentices scurrying underground and impressing on them how crucial safe refuge is while sleet pelts down outside.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to had fun exploring a tunnel together, then ended up sheltering in it when a snowstorm descended.
The tunnels cat_to discovered on an all-apprentice patrol ended up saving cat_from when a snowstorm descended.

success_outcomes.text: The apprentices gather round the weird dead-ended metal tunnel, poking at its rusty sides, clearing away the soil piled on its roof. Really weird, but this'll make a great story to tell back at camp.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to found a strange metal tunnel covered in rust and dirt and had fun exploring it.

success_outcomes.text: The apprentices gather round the weird dead-ended metal tunnel, poking at its rusty sides. s_c suddenly flinches backwards. Get away from that, it's a trap!

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to discovered a Twoleg trap on patrol and had fun messing around with it.
cat_to noticed that a weird tunnel was actually a Twoleg trap and saved cat_from from it.

success_outcomes.text: The tunnels are marked with c_n scent, already known and therefore less enticing. But s_c refuses to leave their safety, arguing with the rest of the patrol until thunder claps outside. The cold rain that would've soaked them all proves {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} point.

log.cats_from: cat_from assumed cat_to was being overly cautious on patrol, right up until a storm descended and cat_to's caution paid off.

success_outcomes.text: The apprentices huddle around the burrow entrance cautiously. But as thunder rolls in over the flat horizon, sending the world into almost night levels of darkness, s_c convinces them to go in — they need shelter, they need the protection of walls around them. They have each other, they're going to be fine.

log.cats_from: cat_from realized what a good leader cat_to would make when {PRONOUN/cat_to/subject} kept every cat calm and dry in a tunnel when a storm descended on their patrol.
cat_from and cat_to huddled in a tunnel together when a thunderstorm descended.

success_outcomes.text: app1 sticks {PRONOUN/app1/poss} head into {PRONOUN/app1/poss} new discovery and finds a furious prairie dog. They exchange blows and chatter at each other, both escaping unscathed and irritated.

log.cats_from: cat_from was impressed when cat_to fought off a prairie dog while on patrol.

fail_outcomes.text: The apprentices gather round the tunnel, investigating. They defer to s_c over whether the tunnel's safe. When s_c nods, app2 scrambles inside — but inside lurks a sleepy wolverine.

history_text.death: m_c was killed when another apprentice made the wrong judgment call on a border patrol.

log.cats_from: cat_to wrongly identified a tunnel as safe, and app2 was killed by a wolverine as a result.
cat_from believed cat_to's negligence on patrol was responsible for app2's death.

fail_outcomes.text: app1 sticks {PRONOUN/app1/poss} head into {PRONOUN/app1/poss} new discovery and finds a furious prairie dog. It delivers a stinging lesson about putting one's head into random holes in the ground.

log.cats_from: cat_from was unimpressed when a prairie dog got the better of cat_to on patrol.

fail_outcomes.text: This burrow is weird. app1's never seen a tunnel with straight walls, or metal ones, for that matter. But c_n can always benefit from knowing about new tunnels, so {PRONOUN/app1/subject} {VERB/app1/creep/creeps} in. The trap door slams shut behind {PRONOUN/app1/object}.

history_text.death: This cat was killed by the shock of watching a fellow apprentice be kidnapped by Twolegs.

fail_outcomes.text: The apprentices gather round the weird dead-ended metal tunnel, poking at its rusty sides, clearing away the soil piled on its roof, crawling in to claw at the back. The trap shuts with a violent snap.

history_text.death: This cat was killed by the shock of watching fellow apprentices be kidnapped by Twolegs.

fail_outcomes.text: s_c bounds forward to the entrance, tail lashing excitedly. There's a shriek, and something pulls {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} in.

history_text.death: This cat was killed after an unfortunate meeting with a wolverine.

fail_outcomes.text: Snow half-blocks the entrance. Ugh. With no one wanting to get their paws chilled digging it out, the patrol continues on. Thunder rumbles threateningly overhead. Sleet and hail await them. The apprentices are found in a huddled, soaked, frostbitten ball of misery, and not all of them can be revived back at camp.

history_text.scar: m_c was frostbitten after going out with a group of friends as apprentices and getting caught in a sleet storm.
history_text.death: m_c was killed after {PRONOUN/m_c/subject} went on a border patrol with {PRONOUN/m_c/poss} friends and got caught in a sleet storm.

fail_outcomes.text: Snow half-blocks the entrance. No one wanting to get their paws chilled digging it out, so the patrol continues on. Thunder rumbles threateningly overhead. Sleet and hail await them. The apprentices are found in a huddled, soaked, frostbitten ball of misery. Their bodies are all cold.
